findings.problem: The chapter keeps stopping to explain why Eli feels real and why Lena is sliding toward him after the concrete beat has already landed. That repeated interpretation makes the seduction legible, but it also softens scene pressure and leaves the second half more argued than dangerous.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or cut the thesis-style explanation between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:237 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:245, between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:297 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:299, between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:411 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:412, between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:449 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:477, and between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:541 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:547. Let at least two of those turns land through consequence instead: the repaired earbud working, the paused Hinge profile, the delayed rush file, or the earlier alarm.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised material between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:237 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:245, between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:411 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:477, and between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:541 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:547 gives each major turn no more than one paragraph of abstract interpretation and ends at least one turn on a concrete consequence rather than a thesis sentence about what Eli means. FAIL if multiple consecutive interpretive paragraphs still arrive after the live beat has already dramatized the shift.

findings.problem: The chapter reuses the same self-betrayal sentence engine four times: Lena knows the prudent move, then the prose flips on an 'Instead' beat. By the third repetition the syntax is announcing the turn before the turn arrives, which makes the surrender feel authored rather than freshly pressured.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite at least two of the pivots at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:93, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:473, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:519 so they do not use the same 'She should have ... Instead ...' reversal. Keep one if you want the pattern once, but make the others break through a different rhythm: direct action, bodily interruption, clipped shame, or a sharper object detail.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:93, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:473, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:519, no more than two pivots use the exact 'She should have ... Instead ...' structure and the remaining pivots turn on visibly different syntax. FAIL if the revised chapter still repeats that same reversal more than twice.

findings.problem: The chapter keeps reaching for the same room-space-weight metaphor to describe desire: his name in the room, the room reweighted, him taking up room, the apartment changing weight, the room staying shaped around him. Because the style bible already privileges spatial pressure, this many near-returns starts to read like token-list adherence rather than fresh perception.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the strongest one or two spatial-pressure images at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:3, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:237, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:411, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:475, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:541, but replace the others with different concrete effects of attachment: a work error, a changed body posture, a household object treated differently, or a social secrecy beat.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, in the revised beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:3, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:237, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:411, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:475, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:541, no more than two passages describe Eli's hold through room-space-weight metaphors and the other passages register the shift through distinct concrete consequences. FAIL if three or more of those beats still rely on the same spatial metaphor family.

summary: Past tense, tight-third focus, continuity, and dialogue register all hold: Lena and Eli stay consistent with the style bible, contractions remain natural, and no continuity contradiction, tense drift, sanitization drift, intimacy fade-to-black, or style-bible example-line reuse surfaced. The chapter lands its required beats and makes Eli socially plausible, but repeated interpretive paragraphs and repeated prose formulas keep award, craft, and prose below pass-level control.
